chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-3897 of 2025 Farhan Naseem Versus Province of Sindh & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections 2. For orders on CMA No.16479/25 3. For hearing of CMA No.15884/25 4. For hearing of main case. 5. For orders on CMA 18594/25 Dated: 15.09.2025 Mr. Faiz H. Durrani, advocate for petitioner. Mr. Dhani Bus Lashari along with Mr. Rafiq Bhanbhro, advocates for Sindh Building Control Authority. -.-.- The petitioner is running a CNG station. He also in addition applied for installing two booths for selling petroleum products. It was allowed by the relevant authorities and therefore he got two booths installed and started selling petroleum products. The Sindh Building Control Authority however sealed the said two booths on the ground that no revised layout plan was submitted for approval of building plan. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he with all the documents went to SBCA office to submit the application on Thursday last for revised layout plan but it was not even accepted and demand of huge bribery was made. Learned counsel for Sindh Building Control Authority has rebutted the same, however has undertaken that he or his clerk will accompany learned counsel for the petitioner and would make sure that the application is received by the office properly. We further direct the relevant authority in the Sindh Building Control Authority to decide the case of the petitioner in accordance with law within 15 days. The petition having served its purpose in above terms is accordingly disposed of, along with listed applications, with the consent of learned counsel for the parties.

We further direct the relevant authority in the Sindh Building Control Authority to decide the case of the petitioner in accordance with law within 15 days. The petition having served its purpose in above terms is accordingly disposed of, along with listed applications, with the consent of learned counsel for the parties. Judge Judge
